title: Allow posting and commenting without a user profile and display authors as “Guest” when no profile exists

requirements.text: Remove the backend profile-existence enforcement for creating posts and adding comments so an authenticated caller can create posts and comments even when they have not created a UserProfile.

quotes: Allow everyone to post irrespective they have profile or not

acceptanceCriteria: Calling `createPost(...)` no longer traps with "User must have a profile to create posts and comments!" when the caller has no stored profile.
Calling `addComment(postId, content)` no longer traps with "User must have a profile to create posts and comments!" when the caller has no stored profile.
Existing authorization checks (e.g., permission checks) remain in place; only the profile-existence requirement is removed for these two actions.

requirements.text: Update backend feed access so users without a profile can still retrieve and view the feed (required to support posting/commenting workflows for users without profiles).

quotes: Allow everyone to post irrespective they have profile or not

acceptanceCriteria: Calling `getFeed()` no longer traps solely because the caller has no profile.
The feed still returns the same Post data as before for callers with profiles.

requirements.text: Update the Post creation UI to allow posting without a profile: remove the UI block/disable logic that prevents composing and submitting posts when `useGetCallerUserProfile()` returns null.
acceptanceCriteria: The post composer textarea, attachment buttons, and Post submit button are usable even when the current user has no profile.
Submitting a post without a profile attempts the backend `createPost` call and shows success/error toasts based on the result (no frontend-side "Please create a profile before posting" hard-block).

requirements.text: Update the Comment creation UI to allow commenting without a profile: remove the UI gating that hides/disables the comment input and submission when the current user has no profile.
acceptanceCriteria: When comments are expanded on a post, the comment input and submit action are available even if the current user has no profile.
Submitting a comment without a profile triggers the existing comment mutation and updates the comments list via existing query invalidation.

requirements.text: Display a placeholder author name of "Guest" for any post or comment whose author does not have a user profile available to the frontend (profile lookup returns null).
acceptanceCriteria: In the feed UI, posts authored by principals without a profile show author name "Guest" instead of "Unknown user" (or a principal placeholder).
In the comments UI, comments authored by principals without a profile show author name "Guest" instead of "Unknown user" (or a principal placeholder).
Avatar fallbacks remain functional for users without profiles (no runtime errors when profile is missing).

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in `backend/main.mo` (no additional backend services).
Do not edit files under `frontend/src/components/ui/*` or the immutable hook/entry files listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT.
nonGoals: Changing posting/commenting permissions for unauthenticated (not signed-in) users.
Implementing a new guest/anonymous identity system; the backend still attributes posts/comments to the caller principal.
Changing profile creation UX beyond removing the posting/commenting gate.
